text: The surgeon advances a thin pediatric nasogastroscope through the colorectal stenosis, extending it approximately thirty centimeters beyond the obstruction. A guide wire is inserted through the nasogastroscope, with its advancement visually monitored to prevent damage to the dilated and partially ischemic colonic wall. The correct position of the guide wire is confirmed using fluoroscopy. The nasogastroscope is then removed, allowing for measurement of the stenosis length to select an appropriately sized stent, while the guide wire remains in place.

A regular size colonoscope is introduced distally to the stenosis, and the stent apparatus is advanced through the guide wire above the stenosis. The progression of the stent apparatus is monitored fluoroscopically and endoscopically. The outer plastic sheath containing the stent is carefully removed, and the stent is positioned correctly with assistance. The guide wire is removed, and the stent's placement is confirmed radiologically. The colonoscope is advanced through the stent to its proximal margin to check for any kinking.
